tests.name: manifest_host_field_populated
tests.details: init.sh wrote --git-host other but manifest.json has no .host field; check-gate.sh --preflight fails until --backfill-host is run; backfill itself requires interactive 'y' confirmation, no non-interactive flag

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh does not copy platform-modules/mcp_server.md to project
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash init.sh --non-interactive --project x --platform mcp_server --deployment organizational --gov-mode production --language python --git-host other --remote-url https://example.com/fake.git --branch-protection-attested --visibility private --project-dir /tmp/x --allow-existing-dir; ls /tmp/x/docs/platform-modules/
framework_bugs.actual: docs/platform-modules/ is empty; verify-install.sh flags 'Platform module (mcp_server.md) missing'

framework_bugs.reproduction: Same init invocation as above; ls .github/workflows/
framework_bugs.expected: release.yml present (per RUNBOOK Section A assertion)

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh leaves manifest.json without .host field; check-gate preflight fails
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run init.sh --git-host other --remote-url ...; jq '.host' .claude/manifest.json -> null. bash scripts/check-gate.sh --preflight -> 'manifest.json missing host field'.
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh writes the chosen git-host into .claude/manifest.json so downstream gates work without an extra --backfill-host step
framework_bugs.actual: host field is absent; user must run check-gate.sh --backfill-host, which itself requires interactive 'y' confirmation (no non-interactive flag)

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh creates docs subdirs with spaces in names
framework_bugs.expected: Directory names should be hyphenated (e.g., adr-documentation, api-and-interfaces) — spaces in dirnames are a usability/scripting hazard
framework_bugs.actual: docs/ADR documentation/ and docs/api and interfaces/ present

documentation_gaps.gap: RUNBOOK assertion 'release_workflow_created' has no scenario-specific guidance — light+mcp_server may legitimately not need release.yml; runbook should call out which configs require it

tests.details: phase1_architecture.steps_completed is array

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 steps (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) marked complete

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh reports 'Phase gates consistent.' rc=0; check-gate.sh --preflight (after --backfill-host) reports 'Not ready: protection verification failed' as expected for fake remote

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D confirmation: no script auto-advances current_phase; .current_phase remained 0 after all phase1 steps + gate

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: Complete all phase1 steps via process-checklist; run check-phase-gate.sh; jq '.current_phase' .claude/phase-state.json -> still 0
framework_bugs.expected: Some script should auto-advance current_phase after the phase1->phase2 gate is satisfied (or runbook should document the manual command)
framework_bugs.actual: current_phase stays at 0 indefinitely; no documented advancement path

framework_bugs.summary: check-gate.sh --backfill-host requires interactive confirmation; no --yes flag
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash scripts/check-gate.sh --backfill-host 0</dev/null -> exits 1 because read prompt fails on closed stdin
framework_bugs.expected: --backfill-host should support a --yes/--auto/--non-interactive flag for scripted use, or init.sh should write the host field directly so backfill is unnecessary
framework_bugs.actual: Only path is `echo y | check-gate.sh --backfill-host`; on EOF stdin it silently aborts with 'Aborted — no changes made'

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmation: direct git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop' SUCCEEDED (rc=0); shell-direct git bypasses PreToolUse hook entirely. Hook would have blocked it had it been routed through PreToolUse.

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1, --check-commit-message 'feat(uat-2): next feature' rc=0 (permitted)

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: With .claude/pending-approval.json present, pre-commit-gate.sh (invoked via PreToolUse JSON stdin) emitted permissionDecision=deny with sentinel reference

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed sentinel; subsequent commit succeeded

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .claude/build-progress.json features_completed contains 'uat-feat-1'

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: direct git commit bypasses BL-006 (PreToolUse only)
framework_bugs.reproduction: git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop' from a clean build_loop state succeeds; only PreToolUse-routed Bash invocations are gated.
framework_bugs.expected: BL-006 enforcement at git commit-msg hook level so shell-direct commits also get blocked
framework_bugs.actual: Direct shell commit creates the commit; gate is purely advisory outside Claude's Bash tool

framework_bugs.summary: pre-commit-gate.sh treats valid sentinel JSON as 'malformed'
framework_bugs.reproduction: Write .claude/pending-approval.json with {version,phase,approved,summary,created_at}; pipe PreToolUse JSON to scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh -> reason includes 'sentinel file ... is malformed. Treated as in flight per the CDF 4.2.3 contract.'
framework_bugs.expected: pre-commit-gate.sh should accept the documented sentinel schema (or the schema should be documented in scripts/pending-approval.sh)
framework_bugs.actual: Even a well-formed sentinel is reported as malformed; the deny still works (correct), but the diagnostic is misleading

documentation_gaps.gap: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh requires a populated HTML file with 'const scenarios = [...]' block; runbook implies it can lint a generic scenario doc

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: First attempt blocked because poc_mode=='production' was treated as a POC mode (see bug below). After running scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production (which set poc_mode=null), --start-phase4 succeeded.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: All 6 phase4_release steps completed (production_build, rollback_tested, go_live_verified, monitoring_configured, handoff_written, handoff_tested) with stub artifacts.

tests.name: handoff_md_required
tests.details: monitoring_configured artifact-check failed until HANDOFF.md included a 'Section 8 Monitoring' subsection; gate then passed.

tests.name: release_notes_required
tests.details: RELEASE_NOTES.md present from init; no gate explicitly required modification but file existed.

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: After all phase4 steps complete, .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase still = 0 (consistent with U-D); no script advances to 4 / 'released'.

framework_bugs.reproduction: Initialize a project with --deployment organizational --gov-mode production. .claude/phase-state.json shows poc_mode='production'. Run scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 -> '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in production mode. POC projects complete at Phase 3. To unlock Phase 4: bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production'
framework_bugs.expected: When poc_mode='production' (or compliance_ready=true after a normal production init), --start-phase4 should proceed without requiring an upgrade.
framework_bugs.actual: scripts/process-checklist.sh:540 treats any non-null poc_mode as a POC mode, including the literal value 'production'. Workaround: run upgrade-project.sh --to-production, which nulls poc_mode and unlocks Phase 4. This forces every production-from-the-start project through a no-op 'upgrade' step.

documentation_gaps.gap: init.sh in production gov-mode writes poc_mode='production' but Phase 4 gating requires poc_mode=null. Either init should set poc_mode=null when gov-mode=production, or process-checklist should accept 'production' as a non-POC value.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: grep finds 'token/secret/api_key' only in framework boilerplate docs (CI workflow, builders-guide, evaluation-prompts) — no actual credentials

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: .claude/process-audit.log absent; no FORCE/reset events occurred during this run (N/A as runbook allows)

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find $WORKDIR/.claude -name '*.tmp' returns nothing

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only pre-existing untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/; framework HEAD unchanged at d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494

summary: Init non-interactive works for production+mcp_server+light. One High bug: --start-phase4 misreads poc_mode='production' as POC and demands a no-op upgrade. Confirmed U-C (direct git bypasses BL-006) and U-D (no current_phase advancement). New Medium bugs: missing platform-modules/mcp_server.md, missing release.yml, manifest.json missing .host field after --git-host other, --backfill-host requires interactive y.
